abstract.t: Active Queue Management (AQM) manages queue depth by controlling packet admission at the point of enqueue. This document specifies a complementary queue management behavior that operates on packets already admitted to a queue: when the queue depth exceeds a configurable threshold, the maximum time a packet may remain queued before being dropped is reduced. When congestion subsides, the duration reverts to its base value. This per-queue dropping behavior helps manage queue depth during congestion by releasing resources from queues where packets have been waiting longest, complementing AQM without modifying its signaling semantics.
